+---
+assignees:
+- wballard
+position_column: todo
+position_ordinal: aa80
+title: Enter on card drills into card fields (parity with Inspector)
+---
+## What
+
+Pressing **Enter** on a focused card is currently a no-op. The user expects the same drill-in behavior the Inspector provides: card → fields → field edit. Today only the second hop (field → edit) and Inspector's drill-in work. Cards never drill into their own fields, so the user can never reach field-edit from a card.
+
+### Root cause
+
+The drill mechanism is genuinely generic — `buildDrillCommands` in `kanban-app/ui/src/components/app-shell.tsx:322-360` installs a single root-level `nav.drillIn` CommandDef that calls `actions.drillIn(focusedFq, focusedFq)`. Cards fail not because the mechanism duplicates logic, but because of a **registration-shape mismatch**:
+
+| | Inspector (works) | Card (broken) |
+|---|---|---|
+| Wrapper | `<FocusZone moniker=…>` (`inspectors-container.tsx:332-352`) | `<FocusScope moniker=…>` (`entity-card.tsx:100-120`) |
+| Kernel call | `spatial_register_zone` (container) | `spatial_register_scope` (leaf) |
+| Field children visible to kernel | yes — FieldRows nest under zone | no — leaf has no descendants; field zones reattach to the column |
+| `spatial_drill_in(self)` returns | first field's FQM | `null` (echoes self) — Enter is a no-op |
+
+Card-as-leaf is **deliberate** (see comment at `entity-card.tsx:58-70`) so that cross-column kernel navigation treats columns as the only zones — flipping it to `<FocusZone>` would break that contract and the kernel test in `swissarmyhammer-focus/tests/navigate.rs` that pins it.
+
+### Fix direction
+
+Mirror the existing `field.edit` pattern (see `kanban-app/ui/src/components/fields/field.tsx:425,516` — a scope-level Enter command that shadows the global `nav.drillIn` for the leaf-with-virtual-children case).
+
+Add a **scope-level `card.drillIn` CommandDef** registered on the card's FocusScope in `entity-card.tsx`. Behavior:
+
+1. On Enter at a focused card, find the first registered field zone whose FQM begins with `field:{entity.type}:{entity.id}.…` (or use the `cardSections` field list to compute the first field's expected moniker).
+2. Dispatch `actions.setFocus(firstFieldFqm)`.
+3. If no field zones exist, fall through (no-op, same as today).
+
+This preserves the card-as-leaf invariant for cross-column nav and gives Enter the parity the user expects.
+
+### Files to modify
+
+- `kanban-app/ui/src/components/entity-card.tsx` — add `card.drillIn` to the `extraCommands` passed to `<FocusScope>` (or compose it into the existing commands prop).
+- `kanban-app/ui/src/components/board-view.enter-drill-in.browser.test.tsx` — **flip** test #1 (`enter_on_focused_card_does_not_dispatch_inspect_in_vim`, ~line 451) which currently pins the no-op as desired behavior; replace with a regression test asserting Enter on a focused card sets focus to the first field zone.
+- Audit `kanban-app/ui/src/components/entity-card.spatial.test.tsx` and `column-view.spatial.test.tsx` for any assertions that pin card-as-no-drill — update only the ones that conflict with the new behavior. Do **not** change the kernel-level card-as-leaf test in `swissarmyhammer-focus/tests/navigate.rs`.
+
+### Non-goals
+
+- Do **not** change `<FocusScope>` to `<FocusZone>` on the card.
+- Do **not** modify the kernel's `spatial_register_scope` / cross-column nav behavior.
+- Do **not** touch `nav.drillIn` in `app-shell.tsx` — the generic handler stays generic; the fix is a localized scope-level shadow, identical in spirit to `field.edit`.
+
+## Acceptance Criteria
+
+- [ ] Pressing Enter on a focused card moves focus to the first field zone of that card (e.g. `field:task:01ABC….title`).
+- [ ] Pressing Enter on the first field then enters edit mode (existing `field.edit` behavior — verify still works in the same flow, no regression).
+- [ ] A card with zero field zones still no-ops on Enter (no error, no focus change).
+- [ ] Cross-column horizontal navigation (left/right between columns) is unchanged — the kernel test in `swissarmyhammer-focus/tests/navigate.rs` still passes unmodified.
+- [ ] No new global `nav.drill*` command is added; the fix is a scope-level command on the card's FocusScope, parallel to `field.edit`.
+
+## Tests
+
+- [ ] **TDD: write the failing browser test first.** Update `kanban-app/ui/src/components/board-view.enter-drill-in.browser.test.tsx` to add a new test: focus a card → press Enter → assert `actions.setFocus` was called with the FQM of the card's first field zone. Run and confirm it fails before implementing.
+- [ ] **Replace existing test #1** (`enter_on_focused_card_does_not_dispatch_inspect_in_vim` ~line 451) since it pins the bug as desired. The replacement asserts the new behavior (Enter drills into first field). Keep the "no inspect dispatch" assertion if still relevant.
+- [ ] Add an end-to-end test in `kanban-app/ui/src/spatial-nav-end-to-end.spatial.test.tsx` (or a new sibling) that walks: column → Enter (drill to card) → Enter (drill to first field) → Enter (drill to edit). All three hops succeed.
+- [ ] Run `bun test board-view.enter-drill-in.browser.test.tsx` and the spatial e2e test — both green.
+- [ ] Run `cargo test -p swissarmyhammer-focus` to confirm kernel tests (cross-column nav, drill semantics) are untouched and green.
+
+## Workflow
+
+- Use `/tdd` — write the failing browser test first, watch it fail, then implement the scope-level `card.drillIn` command. Do not amend existing tests until the new behavior is in place; treat the old test #1 flip as part of the GREEN step.
\ No newline at end of file

changes.value:
## What

Pressing **Enter** on a focused card is currently a no-op. The user expects the same drill-in behavior the Inspector provides: card → fields → field edit. Today only the second hop (field → edit) and Inspector's drill-in work. Cards never drill into their own fields, so the user can never reach field-edit from a card.

### Root cause

The drill mechanism is genuinely generic — `buildDrillCommands` in `kanban-app/ui/src/components/app-shell.tsx:322-360` installs a single root-level `nav.drillIn` CommandDef that calls `actions.drillIn(focusedFq, focusedFq)`. Cards fail not because the mechanism duplicates logic, but because of a **registration-shape mismatch**:

| | Inspector (works) | Card (broken) |
|---|---|---|
| Wrapper | `<FocusZone moniker=…>` (`inspectors-container.tsx:332-352`) | `<FocusScope moniker=…>` (`entity-card.tsx:100-120`) |
| Kernel call | `spatial_register_zone` (container) | `spatial_register_scope` (leaf) |
| Field children visible to kernel | yes — FieldRows nest under zone | no — leaf has no descendants; field zones reattach to the column |
| `spatial_drill_in(self)` returns | first field's FQM | `null` (echoes self) — Enter is a no-op |

Card-as-leaf is **deliberate** (see comment at `entity-card.tsx:58-70`) so that cross-column kernel navigation treats columns as the only zones — flipping it to `<FocusZone>` would break that contract and the kernel test in `swissarmyhammer-focus/tests/navigate.rs` that pins it.

### Fix direction

Mirror the existing `field.edit` pattern (see `kanban-app/ui/src/components/fields/field.tsx:425,516` — a scope-level Enter command that shadows the global `nav.drillIn` for the leaf-with-virtual-children case).

Add a **scope-level `card.drillIn` CommandDef** registered on the card's FocusScope in `entity-card.tsx`. Behavior:

1. On Enter at a focused card, find the first registered field zone whose FQM begins with `field:{entity.type}:{entity.id}.…` (or use the `cardSections` field list to compute the first field's expected moniker).
2. Dispatch `actions.setFocus(firstFieldFqm)`.
3. If no field zones exist, fall through (no-op, same as today).

This preserves the card-as-leaf invariant for cross-column nav and gives Enter the parity the user expects.

### Files to modify

- `kanban-app/ui/src/components/entity-card.tsx` — add `card.drillIn` to the `extraCommands` passed to `<FocusScope>` (or compose it into the existing commands prop).
- `kanban-app/ui/src/components/board-view.enter-drill-in.browser.test.tsx` — **flip** test #1 (`enter_on_focused_card_does_not_dispatch_inspect_in_vim`, ~line 451) which currently pins the no-op as desired behavior; replace with a regression test asserting Enter on a focused card sets focus to the first field zone.
- Audit `kanban-app/ui/src/components/entity-card.spatial.test.tsx` and `column-view.spatial.test.tsx` for any assertions that pin card-as-no-drill — update only the ones that conflict with the new behavior. Do **not** change the kernel-level card-as-leaf test in `swissarmyhammer-focus/tests/navigate.rs`.

### Non-goals

- Do **not** change `<FocusScope>` to `<FocusZone>` on the card.
- Do **not** modify the kernel's `spatial_register_scope` / cross-column nav behavior.
- Do **not** touch `nav.drillIn` in `app-shell.tsx` — the generic handler stays generic; the fix is a localized scope-level shadow, identical in spirit to `field.edit`.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Pressing Enter on a focused card moves focus to the first field zone of that card (e.g. `field:task:01ABC….title`).
- [ ] Pressing Enter on the first field then enters edit mode (existing `field.edit` behavior — verify still works in the same flow, no regression).
- [ ] A card with zero field zones still no-ops on Enter (no error, no focus change).
- [ ] Cross-column horizontal navigation (left/right between columns) is unchanged — the kernel test in `swissarmyhammer-focus/tests/navigate.rs` still passes unmodified.
- [ ] No new global `nav.drill*` command is added; the fix is a scope-level command on the card's FocusScope, parallel to `field.edit`.

## Tests

- [ ] **TDD: write the failing browser test first.** Update `kanban-app/ui/src/components/board-view.enter-drill-in.browser.test.tsx` to add a new test: focus a card → press Enter → assert `actions.setFocus` was called with the FQM of the card's first field zone. Run and confirm it fails before implementing.
- [ ] **Replace existing test #1** (`enter_on_focused_card_does_not_dispatch_inspect_in_vim` ~line 451) since it pins the bug as desired. The replacement asserts the new behavior (Enter drills into first field). Keep the "no inspect dispatch" assertion if still relevant.
- [ ] Add an end-to-end test in `kanban-app/ui/src/spatial-nav-end-to-end.spatial.test.tsx` (or a new sibling) that walks: column → Enter (drill to card) → Enter (drill to first field) → Enter (drill to edit). All three hops succeed.
- [ ] Run `bun test board-view.enter-drill-in.browser.test.tsx` and the spatial e2e test — both green.
- [ ] Run `cargo test -p swissarmyhammer-focus` to confirm kernel tests (cross-column nav, drill semantics) are untouched and green.

## Workflow

- Use `/tdd` — write the failing browser test first, watch it fail, then implement the scope-level `card.drillIn` command. Do not amend existing tests until the new behavior is in place; treat the old test #1 flip as part of the GREEN step.

+Add a **scope-level `card.drillIn` CommandDef** registered on the card's FocusScope in `entity-card.tsx`. Behavior:
